articles.title: Reset FlowSync Teams admin password on Windows (v3.2 and later)
articles.summary: Step-by-step instructions for FlowSync Teams workspace admins to reset the primary admin password on Windows for versions 3.2 and later.
articles.body:
This article explains how FlowSync Teams workspace admins can reset the primary admin password on Windows for versions 3.2 and later.

## Before you start
- Confirm you are using FlowSync Teams version 3.2 or later.
- Ensure you have workspace admin permissions.

## Reset from the sign-in screen
1. On the FlowSync Teams Windows sign-in screen, click **Forgot admin password?**.
2. Enter the admin email address associated with your workspace.
3. Check your inbox for a verification code and enter it in the prompt.
4. Create a new strong password that meets the listed requirements.

## Reset from the admin console
1. Sign in to FlowSync Teams on the web as a workspace owner.
2. Go to **Admin console > Security > Passwords**.
3. Under **Admin password**, click **Reset**.
4. Choose **Send reset link to admin email** or **Force password change on next login**.

## Troubleshooting
- If you do not receive a reset email, review your spam folder and email allowlist.
- If your organization uses SSO, contact your identity provider admin instead.

If you still cannot reset the password, contact FlowSync Support with the error message shown on screen.

articles.title: Reset FlowSync Teams admin password on macOS (v3.2 and later)
articles.summary: Guide for FlowSync Teams admins to reset the primary admin password on macOS devices for version 3.2 and later.
articles.body:
This article covers how to reset the FlowSync Teams admin password on macOS for version 3.2 and later.

## From the macOS desktop app
1. Open the FlowSync Teams desktop app on macOS.
2. On the sign-in screen, click **Forgot admin password?**.
3. Enter the admin email, then click **Send code**.
4. Retrieve the code from your email and enter it when prompted.
5. Create and confirm a new password.

## From the web admin console
Follow the same steps as the Windows article using a browser on macOS.

For SSO-enabled workspaces, password management is controlled by your identity provider.

articles.title: Slack workspace integration setup guide for FlowSync 4.x (workspace admins)
articles.summary: Learn how workspace admins can configure the Slack integration for FlowSync 4.x, including permissions, channels, and security settings.
articles.body:
This guide walks workspace admins through configuring the Slack integration for FlowSync 4.x.

## Prerequisites
- FlowSync workspace on version 4.x or later.
- Slack workspace admin or owner permissions.

## Step 1: Enable Slack integration in FlowSync
1. In FlowSync, go to **Integrations > Slack**.
2. Click **Connect Slack workspace**.
3. Review the requested permissions and click **Allow**.

## Step 2: Configure default channels
1. Choose the default Slack channel for FlowSync notifications.
2. Optionally map FlowSync projects to specific Slack channels.

## Step 3: Set audience and security
1. Limit who can connect personal Slack accounts to FlowSync.
2. Configure message retention and link unfurl settings.

## Verification
Send a test notification from **Integrations > Slack > Send test** to confirm that messages appear in the selected channel.

For troubleshooting steps, see the related Slack troubleshooting article.

articles.title: Slack integration configuration for FlowSync 3.x
articles.summary: Configuration instructions for connecting Slack to FlowSync 3.x workspaces.
articles.body:
This article describes how to configure the Slack integration for FlowSync 3.x.

The steps are similar to FlowSync 4.x but the navigation labels differ slightly. Use this guide if your workspace is still on the 3.x release line.

articles.title: Troubleshooting missing Slack notifications from FlowSync
articles.summary: Diagnose and resolve common reasons why FlowSync updates are not appearing in Slack channels.
articles.body:
If FlowSync updates are not appearing in Slack, use this troubleshooting checklist.

## Check integration status
Confirm that the Slack integration is still connected in **Integrations > Slack**.

## Review channel mappings
Ensure that the project or workspace you expect notifications from is mapped to the correct Slack channel.

## Confirm Slack permissions
If the app was removed or permissions changed, you may need to reconnect the integration.

articles.title: Project templates quickstart for FlowSync Teams (Beginner)
articles.summary: Learn the basics of using project templates in FlowSync Teams, including creating, applying, and customizing beginner-friendly templates.
articles.body:
This beginner-friendly guide introduces project templates in FlowSync Teams.

## What are project templates?
Project templates are reusable blueprints that pre-fill tasks, sections, and automations for common workflows.

## Create a project from a template
1. In FlowSync Teams, click **New project**.
2. Select the **Templates** tab.
3. Choose a template labeled **Beginner** and click **Use template**.

## Customize your template
You can safely rename sections, add tasks, and adjust due dates without changing the original template.

## Recommended starter templates
- Marketing launch (Beginner)
- Team meeting notes (Beginner)

articles.title: Beginner marketing project templates in FlowSync
articles.summary: Overview of beginner-level marketing project templates, including campaign planning and content calendars.
articles.body:
FlowSync includes several beginner-friendly marketing project templates.

## Templates covered
- Campaign launch (Beginner)
- Social media calendar (Beginner)

Each template includes suggested tasks, assignees, and due dates to help teams get started quickly.

articles.title: Remote team project template starter pack (Beginner)
articles.summary: A collection of beginner templates for remote teams, including standups, retrospectives, and onboarding.
articles.body:
Remote teams can use these beginner templates to standardize recurring meetings and workflows.

## Included templates
- Daily standup (Beginner)
- Weekly retrospective (Beginner)
- New hire onboarding (Beginner)

Each template is optimized for distributed teams and includes suggestions for time zones and async updates.

articles.title: Advanced project template automation patterns
articles.summary: For advanced users who want to build highly automated project templates in FlowSync.
articles.body: This article covers advanced automation techniques for project templates, including multi-step rules and cross-project triggers.

articles.title: Resolve FlowSync Desktop sync errors on macOS
articles.summary: Use this guide to fix common FlowSync Desktop sync errors on macOS, including files not updating and offline status issues.
articles.body:
This article is the recommended result for macOS sync errors in the Troubleshooting Wizard.

## Common symptoms
- Files are not updating on your Mac.
- FlowSync Desktop shows **Sync paused** or **Offline**.

## Step 1: Check connectivity
Ensure your Mac is online and that any VPN or proxy settings allow FlowSync traffic.

## Step 2: Restart the sync client
1. Click the FlowSync Desktop menu bar icon.
2. Select **Quit FlowSync Desktop**.
3. Reopen the app from Applications.

## Step 3: Re-authenticate your account
If prompted, sign in again with your FlowSync account.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, start a support chat from this article so an agent can review your logs.

articles.title: Cancel a monthly FlowSync Team Pro plan (US, credit card)
articles.summary: Instructions for canceling a US-based monthly FlowSync Team Pro subscription billed to a credit card, including when charges stop and how to download invoices.
articles.body:
This article explains how to cancel a **monthly FlowSync Team Pro** subscription billed to a **credit card** for customers in the **United States**.

## Before you cancel
- Only billing admins and workspace owners can cancel a subscription.
- Cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period.

## Step 1: Open Billing & Accounts
1. Sign in to FlowSync on the web.
2. Go to **Admin console > Billing & Accounts**.

## Step 2: Select the Team Pro plan
1. In **Plans**, locate **Team Pro**.
2. Confirm that the billing frequency shows **Monthly** and payment method is **Credit card**.

## Step 3: Cancel the subscription
1. Click **Manage plan**.
2. Click **Cancel monthly subscription**.
3. Choose a cancellation reason and click **Confirm cancellation**.

## After cancellation
- Your Team Pro features remain active until the end of the paid month.
- You can export invoices and data before your plan ends.

To keep a copy of these steps, use the **Print-friendly** view at the top of this article.

articles.title: Change FlowSync Team Pro billing from monthly to annual
articles.summary: How to switch a FlowSync Team Pro subscription from monthly to annual billing.
articles.body:
Follow these steps to change your FlowSync Team Pro billing frequency from monthly to annual without interrupting service.

This article applies to all regions and payment methods.

articles.title: Dashboard customization in FlowSync Web
articles.summary: Customize your FlowSync Web dashboard by rearranging widgets, applying filters, and saving views.
articles.body:
This article describes how to customize your dashboard in the FlowSync Web app.

## Reorder widgets
Drag and drop widgets to rearrange them on your dashboard.

## Apply filters
Use the filter bar to show only tasks, projects, or integrations relevant to you.

## Save dashboard layouts
After customizing, click **Save view** and give your layout a name.

For customizing dashboards on the mobile app, see the related article below.

articles.title: Dashboard customization in the FlowSync mobile app
articles.summary: Learn how to customize the dashboard in the FlowSync mobile app, including card order and compact views.
articles.body:
This article explains how to customize your dashboard in the FlowSync mobile app.

## Switch dashboard layout
Choose between compact and expanded card layouts from **Settings > Dashboard**.

## Reorder sections
Tap and hold a section header, then drag it to a new position.

## Sync with web dashboard
Your mobile dashboard preferences can optionally sync with your web dashboard layout.

articles.title: Data export overview for FlowSync workspaces
articles.summary: Overview of all data export options in FlowSync, including CSV exports, full workspace archives, and API-based exports.
articles.body:
This article provides an overview of data export options in FlowSync.

## Export types
- Project-level CSV exports
- Workspace archives
- Automated exports via API

## Permissions
Only workspace admins can start full workspace exports. Project owners can export their own projects.

Use the related articles to learn how to schedule exports and troubleshoot failures.

articles.title: Schedule recurring data exports and reports
articles.summary: How to configure recurring data exports for FlowSync projects and workspaces, including delivery to email or cloud storage.
articles.body:
You can schedule recurring data exports for your FlowSync workspace.

## Create a scheduled export
1. Go to **Data & Export > Scheduled exports**.
2. Click **New scheduled export**.
3. Choose the data scope (workspace or project) and format (CSV or JSON).
4. Select a delivery destination, such as email or cloud storage.

## Manage existing schedules
Edit, pause, or delete scheduled exports from the same page.

articles.title: Troubleshoot failed data exports
articles.summary: Diagnose and fix common reasons why FlowSync data exports fail or time out.
articles.body:
Use this guide to troubleshoot failed or timed-out data exports.

## Common causes
- Export is too large for selected format.
- Destination storage is unavailable or full.
- Permissions changed since the export was scheduled.

## Resolution steps
1. Reduce the export scope or switch to JSON.
2. Confirm that your destination storage is reachable.
3. Re-authenticate any connected storage integrations.

If your export continues to fail, contact Support with the export ID.

articles.title: FlowSync API rate limits and throttling
articles.summary: Developer reference for FlowSync API rate limiting, including per-endpoint limits, burst behavior, and best practices.
articles.body:
This developer reference documents FlowSync API rate limits and throttling behavior.

## Global limits
Describes per-token and per-workspace limits.

## Endpoint-specific limits
Provides tables detailing read and write limits for key endpoints.

## Handling 429 responses
Includes example code snippets in multiple languages that demonstrate exponential backoff and retry strategies.

articles.title: Calendar sync troubleshooting basics for FlowSync
articles.summary: Basic troubleshooting steps when calendar events are not syncing correctly between FlowSync and your external calendar.
articles.body:
If your calendar events are not syncing between FlowSync and your external calendar, start with these basic checks.

## Confirm integration status
1. Go to **Integrations > Calendar**.
2. Ensure your calendar provider is connected.

## Verify calendar selection
Make sure the project is mapped to the correct external calendar.

## Check time zone settings
Mismatched time zones can make events appear missing or shifted.

If the problem persists, proceed to the advanced calendar sync diagnostics article.

articles.title: Advanced calendar sync diagnostics in FlowSync
articles.summary: Advanced diagnostics for ongoing calendar sync failures after completing the basic troubleshooting steps.
articles.body:
Use this article if calendar sync problems continue after following the basic calendar sync troubleshooting guide.

## Collect diagnostic information
1. Note the affected calendar provider and specific calendars.
2. Capture example events that are not syncing.

## Review sync logs
Workspace admins can download sync logs from **Integrations > Calendar > View logs**.

## When to contact support
If issues persist after these steps, use the **Still need help?** link at the bottom of this article to submit a support request. Include details from your diagnostics and the examples you collected.

code_examples.title: Check current rate limit status with cURL
Simple Python client with remaining-quota check
code_examples.code:
import requests

API_TOKEN = "YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN"
BASE_URL = "https://api.flowsync.com/v1"

headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {API_TOKEN}",
    "Accept": "application/json",
}

resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/projects", headers=headers)

# Inspect rate limit headers
limit = resp.headers.get("X-RateLimit-Limit")
remaining = resp.headers.get("X-RateLimit-Remaining")
reset = resp.headers.get("X-RateLimit-Reset")

print(f"Limit: {limit}, Remaining: {remaining}, Resets at: {reset}")

code_examples.title: JavaScript fetch with exponential backoff on 429
Python requests example with 429 handling
code_examples.code:
import time
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://api.flowsync.com/v1"
API_TOKEN = "YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN"

headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {API_TOKEN}",
    "Accept": "application/json",
}

def get_with_retry(path: str, retries: int = 5, backoff: float = 0.5):
    url = f"{BASE_URL}{path}"

    for attempt in range(retries + 1):
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)

        if resp.status_code != 429:
            return resp

        retry_after = resp.headers.get("Retry-After")
        delay = float(retry_after) if retry_after is not None else backoff
        time.sleep(delay)
        backoff *= 2

    raise RuntimeError("Exceeded maximum retries after repeated 429 responses")

response = get_with_retry("/projects")
print(response.status_code, response.json())

code_examples.title: Shell example using jq to inspect rate limit headers
code_examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

API_TOKEN="YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN"

curl -i "https://api.flowsync.com/v1/projects" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${API_TOKEN}" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -o /tmp/flowsync_projects_response.json 2>/tmp/flowsync_headers.txt

# Extract rate limit headers
grep "X-RateLimit" /tmp/flowsync_headers.txt || true
